facts.context: The Hopewell were complex rank societies, but they were not a state. Hopewell culture lacked all of the following elements EXCEPT:
distractors: formal government
urban centers
a permanent military

facts.context: Research on C3 and C4 pathways indicates that changes in plant communities were associated with a transition in the diets of ancient hominids from:
facts.truth: nuts and fruits to species more focused on grasses.
distractors: species focused on grasses to nuts and fruits.
meat to fruits and vegetables.
fruits and vegetables to fish.

facts.context: The Yang-shao culture gave way to the Lung-Shan sometime after:
The sophisticated stone tool making industry at the Pinnacle Point site in South Africa is evidence of:
facts.truth: a 71,000-year-old complex sequence of microblade production and use.
distractors: a 250,000-year-old tradition of hand axe production and use.
an adaptation to hunting in colder environments.
both a and c.

facts.context: Which of the following is a remote-sensing device used at archaeological sites?
distractors: ground-penetrating radar
proton magnetometer
electrical resistivity

facts.context: Which of the following is not a hypothesis for why hominids walked on two legs?
distractors: it freed the hands to carry things
it expended less energy than quadrupedalism for going long distances
it allowed hominids to see greater distances

facts.context: The name Teotihuacán was given to the city by:
facts.truth: the Aztecs, who continued to make pilgrimages there long after its collapse.
distractors: the Spanish invaders, who largely destroyed it.
the city's first king, who named it after his favorite deity.
Olmec traders, who were impressed with its grandeur.

facts.context: Artifacts _________; ecofacts __________
facts.truth: are made by humans; exhibit traces of human activity.
distractors: are valuable and rare; are easy to come by.
decay rapidly; last for a long time.
are facts about art; are facts about nature.

facts.context: The earliest-known use of bronze is found in:
distractors: South America.
Mesopotamia.
the Indus Valley.

facts.context: Unlike Mesopotamian writings, the records left by the Maya are principally concerned with:
distractors: philosophy and religion.
trade and economics.
astronomy and mathematics.

facts.context: How were the royal cemeteries of the Moche discovered?
facts.truth: Thieves raiding the tombs were caught by the police.
distractors: A forest fire burned away the dense vegetation that concealed the cemetery.
Archaeologists discovered an exact replica of the city in the map room.
A flock of sheep that had been living there fell into a tomb.

facts.context: Based on the analysis of oxygen isotope ratios in the shells of gastropods found in the sediments of an ancient lake, what happened to civilization in the Indus Valley?
facts.truth: It eventually collapsed due to climate change and drought.
distractors: It became increasingly complex due to increased rainfall and longer growing seasons.
It collapsed due to increased monsoons and a series of catastrophic floods.
It evolved into a series of city states along rivers and lake shores.

facts.context: What evidence is there that Neandertals were hunters?
facts.truth: Animal bones were found showing stone tool cut marks and no animal teeth marks.
distractors: Neandertal canine teeth showed that they relied only on meat for their subsistence.
Bones of large animals, such as elephant and horse, were found in caves.
Both A and C.

facts.context: Tiwanaku, with its capital at Titicaca, was one of the ___________ kingdoms of the ancient world.
distractors: richest
strongest
largest

facts.context: The Lake Forest Archaic tradition relied on ____________ resources; the Maritime Archaic hunted ___________ creatures.
distractors: pelagic; midden
midden; littoral
littoral; lacustrine

facts.context: Which best describes Stonehenge?
distractors: Halafian monumental center
Chavin ritual monument
Mesopotamian monument

facts.context: The fact that early excavators of Teotihuacán mistook ordinary housing compounds for palaces suggests:
facts.truth: the general standard of living was high.
distractors: commoners took over the residences of the elite.
the city was a democracy, ruled by common farmers and artisans.
the society was mostly egalitarian, with almost no social stratification.

facts.context: The large, extinct herbivores of the Pleistocene era are referred to as:
distractors: megaliths.
megaflora.
megatrons.

facts.context: Recovery of more than 6,500 bones and bone fragments from 28 individuals of a single hominid species at the Cave of the Bones in Spain provides:
facts.truth: unusually good evidence to examine diversity within a hominid species.
distractors: little evidence of diversity, since each individual represents a different species.
evidence of interbreeding between Homo neanderthalensis and Homo erectus.
relatively little evidence to examine diversity within the species.

facts.context: Recent discoveries of Homo naledi fossils in the Dinaledi Chamber of the Rising Star cave system in South Africa suggest a mixture of post-cranial morphologies or mosaic of hominid traits. One of the reasons this new species is controversial is that:
facts.truth: there is, as yet, no firm date that can be applied to the specimens.
distractors: there are already two well-known species of Homo and it is unlikely there were more than two species at one time.
paleoanthropologists did not make the discovery and it was found in an inaccessible cave in South Africa.
the Homo naledi fossils have been shown to pre-date the earliest known hominid fossils by millions of years.

facts.context: Based on the strontium isotope ratios in the teeth of people buried at Copán, who were the rulers?
facts.truth: foreigners from different regions across much of the Maya territory
distractors: local elite who rose to power based on their popularity and knowledge of politics
an elite class of astronomer-priests who ate mostly vegetables and fish
Egyptian emigrants who arrived on ships and taught the Maya how to build pyramids

facts.context: What was found in some of the burials at the principal Liangzhu site in eastern China that represents elite status?
facts.truth: finely crafted large jade disks called bi
distractors: thousands of shell discs called “bi” that were used as a source of money
copper earspools that were worn by the deceased
gold headdresses that were worn by the deceased

facts.context: Analysis of the teeth of a hominid species discovered in Malapa Cave in South Africa, dating just after 2 million years ago, indicates a diet that consisted primarily of:
distractors: meat derived from carnivorous mammals.
meat, fish, and some plants.
C4 grasses that may have included wild wheat.

facts.context: Homo habilis emerged around ________ years ago; Homo erectus followed at around _________ years ago.
distractors: 4.5 million; 3.2 million
3.6 million; 2.8 million

facts.context: The fossils originally known as Sinanthropus pekinensis or “Peking Man” are now classified as:
distractors: Homo floresiensis.
Homo sapiens.
Homo habilis.

facts.context: When did Mohenjo-daro develop into a complex urban center?
facts.truth: during the Mature Harappan period, from 4,500 to 4,000 B.P.
distractors: during the Early Shang Dynasty, from 1,000 to 400 B.P.
during the Qin Dynasty, from 3,500 to 3,000 B.P.
during the Early Harappan period, from 8,500 to 6,000 B.P.

facts.context: The Upper Paleolithic is generally associated with:
distractors: new and improved stone tool technologies.
a broadening of the subsistence base.
larger sites with increased populations.

facts.context: The key factor in the survival and success of anatomically modern human beings was:
distractors: their ability to control fire and produce art.
their ability to interbreed with other hominids.
their ability to hunt large mammals.

facts.context: Which area is home to the tradition of making stone tools from chipped pebbles, called Hoabinhian?
distractors: Australia
North America
Europe

facts.context: Archaeological evidence for the production of cheese and yogurt in Europe suggests that dairying became an important part of subsistence by ______ years ago, with _______ providing an adaptive advantage.
distractors: 10,000; artificial selection for certain varieties of cheese
5,500; domesticated cattle
3,500; certain strains of lactose-free cheese and yogurt
